project.name: rais-web
project.description: RAIS Pro — an enterprise manufacturing intelligence dashboard (Next.js 16 + React 19 + AI SDK v6) that ingests factory rejection/production Excel reports, classifies columns with LLM assistance, computes quality metrics deterministically, and renders executive KPI dashboards with SPC charts. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: global-ontology.ts

nodes.name: exact-index.ts
nodes.summary: Builds an exact-match lookup index over previously verified mods so repeat workbook uploads resolve instantly without re-running the ladder.

nodes.name: ladder.ts

nodes.name: conceptsForRole

nodes.name: globalHit
nodes.summary: Attempts an exact or synonym match against the global ontology seed, the highest-confidence ladder rung.

nodes.name: ruleHit
nodes.summary: Applies heuristic pattern rules (substrings, role hints) to match a header to a concept when no exact hit exists.

nodes.name: toProposal
nodes.summary: Wraps a resolution hit into a MappingProposal with confidence, basis, and alternatives.

nodes.name: resolveWorkbook

nodes.name: knowledge-store.ts

nodes.name: getKnowledgeStore

nodes.name: formula-class.ts

nodes.name: from-workbook.ts

nodes.name: profile.ts

nodes.name: dominantFormulaClass

nodes.summary: Computes stable structural signatures (hashed normalized headers) for workbooks so identical layouts can be matched to previously verified mods.

nodes.name: stableHash
nodes.summary: Deterministic string hash used as the basis for workbook signatures.

nodes.name: normalizeName
nodes.summary: Normalizes header names (case, whitespace, punctuation) before signature hashing.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the profiler layer: ProfilingTable, ColumnProfile, column roles, and formula classes shared across profiler and resolver.

nodes.name: header.ts
nodes.summary: Header detection utilities for messy factory spreadsheets: date-likeness checks, header-row scoring, multi-row header block assembly, and column label conversion.

nodes.name: isDateLike

nodes.name: normalizeHeaders

nodes.name: colIndexToLabel

nodes.name: buildHeaderBlock

nodes.summary: Central Zod schema contract for all AI structured output: sheet graphs, sheet mappings, dashboard config, merge plans, and narrative — written for cross-provider strict-mode compatibility (nullable over optional, plain ints).

nodes.name: d1.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical D1 data contract: type definitions for provenance, confidence, client registry (stages/sizes/defects), and the full append-only CanonicalEvent union (production, inspection, rejection, carryover, correction, annotation, dispatch).
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type/interface module forming the schema contract shared by ingest, stores, and analytics; the discriminated CanonicalEvent union is the backbone of the append-only event store.

nodes.name: SupabaseFindingStore
nodes.summary: Persists audit findings with adjudication verdicts and derived finding state.

nodes.name: SupabaseRulebookStore
nodes.summary: Stores rulebook rules and records rule applications against events.

nodes.name: SupabaseRegistryStore
nodes.summary: CRUD store for client registries including active-registry selection and renames.

nodes.name: toRegistryRow
nodes.summary: Maps raw Supabase row data into a typed registry row.

nodes.summary: Supabase client factory providing lazily created browser and server clients from environment credentials.

nodes.name: getSupabaseClient

nodes.name: createServerClient

nodes.summary: POST /api/decide route: validates a DecideRequest, canonicalizes stored events, runs the deterministic decision engine, and optionally attaches LLM explanations to the recommendations.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: CAPA (corrective/preventive action) page: fetches decision-engine recommendations from /api/decide, lets users promote them into tracked actions with owner, due date, priority, and status.

nodes.name: decision-engine.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the decision engine: builds a high-rejection event corpus and asserts seed rules fire with correct canonical variables, templates, and severity ordering.

nodes.name: canonical-vars.ts
nodes.summary: Computes the flat canonical variable map (rejection rate, FPY, COPQ, per-stage/defect/size metrics and formatted display strings) that decision-rule predicates and templates evaluate against.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles decide requests: Zod-parses the body, loads effective events from the store, resolves the active mod catalog, runs decide(), and returns recommendations with optional AI explanations.

nodes.name: computeCanonicalVars
nodes.summary: Aggregates scoped events through the analytics layer (rejectionRate, fpy, byStage, byDefect, bySize, copq) into a single flat variable record with dotted per-dimension keys and preformatted display values.

nodes.summary: Deterministic decision engine: evaluates rule predicates against canonical variables, fills recommendation templates, and returns severity-sorted recommendations. No AI involvement.

nodes.name: explain.ts

nodes.languageNotes: Follows the project invariant that AI only narrates; all numeric values are passed read-only into the prompt.

nodes.name: rule-store.ts
nodes.summary: Versioned decision-rule persistence with dual backends: an in-memory store seeded from SEED_DECISION_RULES and a Supabase-backed store, selected via shouldUseSupabase().

nodes.name: seed-rules.ts
nodes.summary: Seed catalog of built-in decision rules (rejection-rate breaches, stage/defect/size concentration, COPQ thresholds) expressed as predicate + templated recommendation definitions.

nodes.name: rejection.registry.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests that rejection aggregation respects a custom client registry, using a custom stage record and emitted events.

nodes.name: scope.test.ts

nodes.name: cost.ts
nodes.summary: Cost-of-poor-quality analytics: COPQ, savings opportunity vs. a target rejection rate, and COPQ trend per period, with per-unit cost and stage weights read from localStorage settings.

nodes.name: defect.ts
nodes.summary: Defect analytics: Pareto-style byDefect totals with registry labels, top-N defect trends per period, and size-level rejection breakdowns.

nodes.name: narrative.ts
nodes.summary: Builds a compact narrative context object (rates, totals, top defects/stages) that is handed to the AI chat/narrative layer as read-only facts.

nodes.summary: Core rejection analytics over canonical events: aggregate totals, rejection rate, FPY, per-stage/size breakdowns, and daily/weekly/cumulative trend series, all pure deterministic arithmetic.

nodes.name: evalPredicate
nodes.summary: Evaluates a single rule predicate (op + threshold) against a numeric canonical variable, treating missing/NaN values as non-matching.

nodes.name: whenMatches
nodes.summary: Checks a rule's when-clause: every all-predicate must hold and, if present, at least one any-predicate.

nodes.name: fillTemplate
nodes.summary: Substitutes {var} placeholders in recommendation templates with formatted canonical values (percent formatting for rate keys, fixed decimals for floats).

nodes.name: matchedVars
nodes.summary: Collects the subset of canonical variables referenced by a rule's predicates for inclusion in the recommendation payload.

nodes.name: decide
nodes.summary: Main engine entry: computes canonical vars for the scoped events, matches active rules from the rule store, fills templates, and returns recommendations sorted by severity then ruleId.

nodes.name: explainRecommendations
nodes.summary: Asks the AI provider chain (tryModels + generateObject) for 1-2 sentence explanations per fired rule, with strict prompt constraints against introducing new numbers.

nodes.summary: React context providing live theme tweaks (density, background, card style, chart style, dark/light) that are painted onto document body data-attributes and persisted to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads the pre-hydration theme from a data-attribute set on documentElement to avoid a flash of wrong theme.

nodes.name: TweaksProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider that holds tweak state, syncs the theme to the document element and localStorage, and exposes setters via memoized callbacks.

nodes.name: useTweaks
nodes.summary: Hook that returns the current tweaks context for consumers across the app's pages.

nodes.name: empty-registry.ts
nodes.summary: Exports EMPTY_REGISTRY, a registry-shaped empty ontology catalog that pages fall back to when no verified manufacturing ontology exists, so the UI shows an empty state instead of a hardcoded company.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file forming the analytics engine's public API; screens import scope, canonical, rejection, defect, size, cost, trust, status, and narrative modules exclusively through it.

nodes.name: pareto.ts
nodes.summary: Pure Pareto (80/20) analysis over defect data: sorts contributions, computes cumulative percentages, and identifies the vital-few defects with a human-readable insight string.

nodes.name: calculatePareto
nodes.summary: Filters and sorts labeled values, computes per-item and cumulative percentages, flags the vital-few crossing 80%, and produces a summary insight sentence.

nodes.name: audit-package.ts
nodes.summary: Builds a downloadable audit evidence ZIP entirely client-side: CSV exports of KPIs, stage/defect/size breakdowns and trends, the raw event log, and a SHA-256 manifest, using a hand-rolled store-only ZIP writer.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the ZIP local/central-directory format manually with DataView (no compression) and uses Web Crypto for per-file SHA-256 hashes.

nodes.name: makeZip
nodes.summary: Assembles a store-method ZIP archive from named byte entries by writing local file headers, a central directory, and the end-of-central-directory record.

nodes.name: buildAuditPackage
nodes.summary: Runs the analytics engine over the event log for a scope, renders KPI/stage/defect/size/trend CSVs plus the raw events, hashes each file into a manifest, and returns the zipped audit package as a Blob.

nodes.name: chart-utils.ts

nodes.name: getBaseSpacing
nodes.summary: Returns the pixel spacing between chart points based on how many points must fit.

nodes.name: useContainerWidth
nodes.summary: React hook that tracks a container element's width with a ResizeObserver so SVG charts can re-layout responsively.

nodes.name: hoverIndexFromPixels
nodes.summary: Converts a mouse clientX position into the nearest chart point index, clamped to the valid range.

nodes.name: shouldShowLabel
nodes.summary: Decides whether an axis label should render for a point, thinning labels by spacing and time grain to avoid clutter.

nodes.name: dashboard.ts

nodes.name: 001_sessions.sql
nodes.summary: Initial migration creating the sessions and insight_slides tables that persist uploaded-file dashboard sessions and AI-generated insight slides, keyed by device_id, with lookup indexes.

nodes.name: sessions

nodes.name: insight_slides

nodes.name: 002_add_merge_plan.sql

nodes.languageNotes: RLS is enabled but all policies use `using (true)`, so access is effectively open despite the device_id-named policies.

nodes.name: 20260615000000_schema.sql
nodes.summary: Core canonical-ledger schema: raw file storage, ingestions, the append-only events ledger, findings/adjudications/rulebook governance tables, per-client registries, and cost configuration, with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: raw_files
nodes.summary: Content-addressed storage of uploaded Excel files keyed by file_hash, keeping original bytes for provenance and re-processing.

nodes.name: ingestions
nodes.summary: Records each file ingestion run, linking an ingestion_id to the source file name and hash.

nodes.name: events

nodes.name: findings
nodes.summary: Data-quality findings raised by rules against ingestions, with severity, evidence, hypotheses, and GM-authority flags.

nodes.name: adjudications
nodes.summary: Human/AI verdicts on findings, optionally linked to a correction event, forming the governance feedback loop.

nodes.name: rulebook_rules
nodes.summary: Versioned rulebook rules with predicate/action definitions, lifecycle status, and lineage back to the adjudications they were born from.

nodes.name: rule_applications
nodes.summary: Join table recording which rulebook rule version was applied to which finding and ingestion, and when.

nodes.name: registries

nodes.name: cost_config

nodes.name: 20260618_canonical_ledger.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent re-baseline of the canonical ledger (events, findings, adjudications, rulebook_rules, rule_applications) plus legacy sessions and dashboards tables, ensuring the schema exists on fresh Supabase projects.
nodes.languageNotes: Largely duplicates 20260615000000_schema.sql table shapes using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, acting as a defensive baseline rather than a delta.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Baseline definition of the append-only events ledger with provenance, confidence, and supersession columns, created if absent.

nodes.name: findings

nodes.name: adjudications

nodes.name: rule_applications

nodes.name: dashboards

tour.title: Project Overview & Conventions
tour.description: Start with the README and AGENTS.md to understand what RAIS Pro is — an enterprise manufacturing-intelligence OS that turns factory Excel reports into quality dashboards — and the hard rules governing the AI pipeline (the model classifies and narrates; it never does maths).

tour.title: App Shell & Boot
tour.description: How the Next.js App Router boots: the root layout and landing page mount the AppShell, which wires up the registry, events, and page-loading contexts shared across every screen.

tour.title: Ingesting a Workbook
tour.description: The first stage of the pipeline: UploadZone hands a spreadsheet to the workbook reader and header detector, which turn raw cells into a structured, profiled workbook.

tour.title: Ontology & Column Understanding
tour.description: How columns get meaning: the ontology mod-builder and the resolver ladder (exact index → LLM fallback) classify each column, backed by the knowledge store of learned mods.

tour.title: The AI Provider Chain
tour.description: Every AI call flows through tryModels in ai.ts, which walks Gateway → Anthropic → OpenRouter → Google → Groq → Ollama. Zod schemas in schemas.ts are the cross-provider contract for classification and narrative output.

tour.title: Deterministic Analytics
tour.description: All numbers come from pure JS: the analytics modules compute rejection rate, cost of poor quality, defect Pareto, and trust scoring over the raw rows — never from the model.

tour.title: The Decision Engine
tour.description: Reconciled data is run through a rule-based decision engine that emits explainable pass/reject/flag decisions over canonical variables.

tour.title: Persistence Layer
tour.description: How state survives: the store index and Supabase adapter persist sessions, mods, and append-only events; the SQL migrations define the underlying schema and RLS.

tour.title: Rendering the Dashboard
tour.description: The payoff: the editorial charts, Pareto chart, and insight/detail components render KPIs and SPC-style trends. The main dashboard page composes them into the executive view.

tour.title: API Surface
tour.description: Wrapping up: the route handlers that tie it together — ingest for staging manual entries and workbooks, mods for column-mapping, and events for the audit trail.
